    <h1 {...{
      "id": "resize-elements"
    }}>{`Resize Elements`}</h1>
    <p>{`Adobe Express provides a powerful unified API for resizing elements. The `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../references/document-sandbox/document-apis/classes/Node.md#resize"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`resize()`}</inlineCode></a>{` method gives you fine-grained control over how elements change size, whether they maintain aspect ratios, and how visual details like strokes and corners are affected.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert slots="text" variant="warning" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`IMPORTANT:`}</strong>{` This API is currently `}<strong parentName="p"><em parentName="strong">{`experimental only`}</em></strong>{` and should not be used in any add-ons you will be distributing until it has been declared stable. To use it, you will first need to set the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`experimentalApis`}</inlineCode>{` flag to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`true`}</inlineCode>{` in the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../references/manifest/index.md#requirements"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`requirements`}</inlineCode></a>{` section of the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "understanding-resize-behavior"
    }}>{`Understanding Resize Behavior`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`resize()`}</inlineCode>{` method accepts a `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../references/document-sandbox/document-apis/type-aliases/ResizeOptions.md"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`ResizeOptions`}</inlineCode></a>{` object with three mandatory components:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Dimensions`}</strong>{`: Specify `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`width`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`height`}</inlineCode>{`, or both (depending on the behavior).`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Behavior`}</strong>{`: Controls aspect ratio handling via `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../references/document-sandbox/document-apis/enumerations/ResizeBehavior.md"
        }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`ResizeBehavior`}</inlineCode></a>{` enumeration.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Visual Details`}</strong>{`: Choose whether to scale strokes, corners detailing, etc.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "resize-behaviors"
    }}>{`Resize Behaviors`}</h3>
    <p>{`Defined in the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../references/document-sandbox/document-apis/enumerations/ResizeBehavior.md"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`ResizeBehavior`}</inlineCode></a>{` enumeration.`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`ResizeBehavior.contain`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Fits the element entirely within the specified dimensions.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`ResizeBehavior.cover`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Ensures the element completely covers the specified area.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`ResizeBehavior.proportional`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Maintains the element's aspect ratio.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <InlineAlert slots="text" variant="info" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Dimension requirements:`}</strong>{` `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ResizeBehavior.proportional`}</inlineCode>{` requires only one dimension (never both); `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ResizeBehavior.contain`}</inlineCode>{` can use one or both dimensions; `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ResizeBehavior.cover`}</inlineCode>{` typically uses both dimensions.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "visual-details-flag"
    }}>{`Visual Details Flag`}</h3>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible`}</inlineCode>{` flag determines how visual styling is handled:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`true`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Preserves stroke widths, corner radii, etc.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`false`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Scales all visual properties proportionally with the element.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "proportional-resizing"
    }}>{`Proportional Resizing`}</h2>
    <p>{`Proportional resizing maintains the element's aspect ratio while changing its size. Specify either `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`width`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`height`}</inlineCode>{` (never both) and the other dimension will adjust automatically.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-resize-by-width"
    }}>{`Example: Resize by Width`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// sandbox/code.js
import { editor, colorUtils, constants } from "express-document-sdk";

// Create a rectangle
const rect = editor.createRectangle();
rect.width = 200;
rect.height = 100;
rect.translation = { x: 100, y: 100 };
rect.fill = editor.makeColorFill(colorUtils.fromHex("#3498db"));

editor.context.insertionParent.children.append(rect);

// Resize proportionally by width, scaling all visual details
rect.resize({
  width: 300,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.proportional,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: false
});

console.log(\`New dimensions: \${rect.width} x \${rect.height}\`);
// New dimensions: 300 x 150
`}</code></pre>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-resize-by-height"
    }}>{`Example: Resize by Height`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// sandbox/code.js
import { editor, colorUtils, constants } from "express-document-sdk";

const ellipse = editor.createEllipse();
ellipse.rx = 100; // radius x = 100 (width = 200)
ellipse.ry = 50;  // radius y = 50 (height = 100)
ellipse.translation = { x: 150, y: 100 };
ellipse.fill = editor.makeColorFill(colorUtils.fromHex("#F0B76C"));

editor.context.insertionParent.children.append(ellipse);

// Resize proportionally by height, scaling all visual details
ellipse.resize({
  height: 150,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.proportional,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: false
});

console.log(\`New bounds: \${ellipse.boundsLocal.width} x \${ellipse.boundsLocal.height}\`);
// New bounds: 300 x 150
`}</code></pre>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-preserve-visual-details"
    }}>{`Example: Preserve Visual Details`}</h3>
    <p>{`When `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible`}</inlineCode>{` is set to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`true`}</inlineCode>{`, strokes and other visual details maintain their original size:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// sandbox/code.js
import { editor, colorUtils, constants } from "express-document-sdk";

const rect = editor.createRectangle();
rect.width = 100;
rect.height = 100;
rect.translation = { x: 100, y: 100 };
rect.fill = editor.makeColorFill(colorUtils.fromHex("#3498db"));

// Add a 5px stroke
const stroke = editor.makeStroke({
  color: colorUtils.fromHex("#2c3e50"),
  width: 5,
  position: constants.StrokePosition.inside
});
rect.stroke = stroke;

editor.context.insertionParent.children.append(rect);

// Resize proportionally while preserving stroke width
rect.resize({
  width: 200,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.proportional,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true     // preserves stroke width
  // avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: false // scales stroke width
});

console.log(\`Stroke width after resize: \${rect.stroke.width}px\`);
// Stroke width after resize: 5px (unchanged)
`}</code></pre>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "contain-behavior"
    }}>{`Contain Behavior`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`contain`}</inlineCode>{` behavior fits an element entirely within specified dimensions, similar to CSS `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`object-fit: contain`}</inlineCode>{`. Elements with fixed aspect ratios may leave unused space on one axis.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-contain-within-bounds-shapes"
    }}>{`Example: Contain Within Bounds (Shapes)`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// sandbox/code.js
import { editor, colorUtils, constants } from "express-document-sdk";

const ellipse = editor.createEllipse();
ellipse.rx = 100; // radius x = 100 (width = 200)
ellipse.ry = 50;  // radius y = 50 (height = 100)
ellipse.translation = { x: 150, y: 100 };
ellipse.fill = editor.makeColorFill(colorUtils.fromHex("#F0B76C"));

editor.context.insertionParent.children.append(ellipse);
// Fit within a 150x150 box
ellipse.resize({
  width: 150,
  height: 150,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.contain,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
});

console.log(\`Resized dimensions: \${ellipse.width} x \${ellipse.height}\`);
// Resized dimensions: 150 x 150
`}</code></pre>

    <InlineAlert slots="header, text" variant="warning" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Shapes and Aspect Ratio`}</p>
    <p>{`Please note that shapes are resized to fit within a bounding box `}<strong parentName="p">{`disregarding their aspect ratio`}</strong>{`; they are free to extend or shrink on both axes.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-contain-with-one-dimension-shapes"
    }}>{`Example: Contain with One Dimension (Shapes)`}</h3>
    <p>{`You can specify only one dimension with `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`contain`}</inlineCode>{` behavior to adjust that dimension while ideally keeping the other fixed:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// sandbox/code.js
import { editor, colorUtils, constants } from "express-document-sdk";

const ellipse = editor.createEllipse();
ellipse.rx = 100;
ellipse.ry = 75;
ellipse.translation = { x: 150, y: 150 };
ellipse.fill = editor.makeColorFill(colorUtils.fromHex("#e74c3c"));

editor.context.insertionParent.children.append(ellipse);

// Adjust width while attempting to preserve height
ellipse.resize({
  width: 300,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.contain,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
});

console.log(\`New bounds: \${ellipse.boundsLocal.width} x \${ellipse.boundsLocal.height}\`);
`}</code></pre>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-contain-within-bounds-media"
    }}>{`Example: Contain Within Bounds (Media)`}</h3>
    <p>{`Media elements (images, videos) maintain their aspect ratio; cropping may happen when the aspect ratio of the media does not match the aspect ratio of the container.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// sandbox/code.js

// Assuming the user has selected an Image node

console.log(
  "Initial dimensions: " +
    imageNode.boundsLocal.width +
    " x " +
    imageNode.boundsLocal.height
);
// Initial dimensions: 300 x 200

imageNode.resize({
  width: 150,
  height: 150,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.contain,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
});
console.log(
  "Resized dimensions: " +
    imageNode.boundsLocal.width +
    " x " +
    imageNode.boundsLocal.height
);
// Resized dimensions: 150 x 150
`}</code></pre>

    <InlineAlert slots="text, text1" variant="warning" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Note that the resize behavior for `}<strong parentName="p">{`media elements`}</strong>{` can differ significantly from shapes, especially when `}<strong parentName="p">{`cropping`}</strong>{` is involved.`}</p>
    <p>{`This is because the `}<strong parentName="p">{`container`}</strong>{` is resized to fit within the bounding box, while the `}<strong parentName="p">{`media`}</strong>{` inside the container also needs to be adjusted to avoid blank space between the two.`}</p>
    <p>{`For instance, in the next example we’ll start with the 150x150 image we resized earlier, trying to fit it within a 300x150 box. The results can be counterintuitive, since resize, under the hood, uses a combination of steps to achieve the final dimensions — but it lacks the contextual understanding that Adobe Express has when users interact with the UI. This means that API-based resize calls should always be validated afterward to ensure they produce the expected outcome.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// sandbox/code.js
import { editor, constants } from "express-document-sdk";

// Assuming the user has selected an Image node
const imageNode = editor.context.selection[0];

console.log(
  "Initial dimensions: " +
    imageNode.boundsLocal.width +
    " x " +
    imageNode.boundsLocal.height
);
// Initial dimensions: 150 x 150

// Resize to fit within a 300x150 box
imageNode.resize({
  width: 300,
  height: 150,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.contain,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
});

console.log(
  "Resized dimensions: " +
    imageNode.boundsLocal.width +
    " x " +
    imageNode.boundsLocal.height
);
// Resized dimensions: 300 x 150
`}</code></pre>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "cover-behavior"
    }}>{`Cover Behavior`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`cover`}</inlineCode>{` behavior ensures an element completely covers the specified area, similar to CSS `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`object-fit: cover`}</inlineCode>{`. Elements with fixed aspect ratios may extend beyond the target bounds on one axis.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-cover-an-area"
    }}>{`Example: Cover an Area`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// sandbox/code.js
import { editor, colorUtils, constants } from "express-document-sdk";

// Assuming the user has selected an Image node
const imageNode = editor.context.selection[0];

console.log(
  "Initial dimensions: " +
    imageNode.boundsLocal.width +
    " x " +
    imageNode.boundsLocal.height
);
// Initial dimensions: 150 x 100

// Resize to cover a 120x120 area
imageNode.resize({
  width: 120,
  height: 120,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.cover,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
});

console.log(
  "Covered dimensions: " +
    imageNode.boundsLocal.width +
    " x " +
    imageNode.boundsLocal.height
);
// Covered dimensions: 120 x 120
`}</code></pre>

    <p>{`In this example, the image is resized to cover a 120x120 area; the image is enlarged maintaining its aspect ratio, while the crop is adjusted to fit within the bounding box.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert slots="text" variant="info" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`With nodes that can resize irrespective of their aspect ratio, like shapes, the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ResizeBehavior.cover`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ResizeBehavior.contain`}</inlineCode>{` methods yield the same result.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "working-with-text-elements"
    }}>{`Working with Text Elements`}</h2>
    <p>{`Text elements adjust their font size when resized. Both proportional and contain/cover behaviors produce similar results:`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-resize-text-proportionally"
    }}>{`Example: Resize Text Proportionally`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// sandbox/code.js
import { editor, constants } from "express-document-sdk";

const textNode = editor.createText("Hello, World!");

const insertionParent = editor.context.insertionParent;
textNode.setPositionInParent(
  { x: insertionParent.width / 2, y: insertionParent.height / 2 },
  { x: 0, y: 0 }
);

insertionParent.children.append(textNode);

// Resize text proportionally - font size scales accordingly
textNode.resize({
  width: 300,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.proportional,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: false
});
`}</code></pre>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-equivalent-resize-behaviors-for-text"
    }}>{`Example: Equivalent Resize Behaviors for Text`}</h3>
    <p>{`As we've seen, when resizing text the font size is adjusted to fit within the specified bounds. Different combinations of `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ResizeBehavior`}</inlineCode>{` parameters can produce equivalent results, as shown below.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// sandbox/code.js
// import { editor } from "express-document-sdk";

// Assuming the user has selected a text frame
const textNode = editor.context.selection[0];

// Both will result in a 200px wide text
textNode.resize({
   width: 200, height: 100,
   behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.contain,
   avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
})
textNode.resize({
   width: 200,
   behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.proportional,
   avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
})

// Both will result in a 100px tall text
textNode.resize({
   width: 200, height: 100,
   behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.cover,
   avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
})
textNode.resize({
   height: 100,
   behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.proportional,
   avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
})
`}</code></pre>
    <p><span parentName="p" {...{
        "className": "gatsby-resp-image-wrapper",
        "style": {
          "position": "relative",
          "display": "block",
          "marginLeft": "auto",
          "marginRight": "auto",
          "maxWidth": "1280px"
        }
      }}>{`
      `}<span parentName="span" {...{
          "className": "gatsby-resp-image-background-image",
          "style": {
            "paddingBottom": "42.8125%",
            "position": "relative",
            "bottom": "0",
            "left": "0",
            "display": "block",
            "transition": "opacity 0.5s 0.5s",
            "pointerEvents": "none"
          }
        }}></span>{`
  `}<picture parentName="span">{`
          `}<source parentName="picture" {...{

    <p>{`In the screenshot above, the original text is top-left; top-right, the result of both `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`resize()`}</inlineCode>{` with `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`contain`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`proportional`}</inlineCode>{` behavior (width-only); bottom, the result of both `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`resize()`}</inlineCode>{` with `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`cover`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`proportional`}</inlineCode>{` behavior (height-only).`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "working-with-pages"
    }}>{`Working with Pages`}</h2>
    <p>{`Page resizing uses a simpler approach; simply set the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`width`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`height`}</inlineCode>{` properties:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// sandbox/code.js
import { editor } from "express-document-sdk";

const page = editor.documentRoot.pages.first;
console.log(\`Page dimensions before: \${page.width} x \${page.height}\`);

page.width  = 300;
page.height = 300;

console.log(\`Page dimensions after: \${page.width} x \${page.height}\`);
// Page dimensions before: 400 x 600
// Page dimensions after: 300 x 300
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "common-patterns"
    }}>{`Common Patterns`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "resize-to-exact-dimensions-shapes"
    }}>{`Resize to Exact Dimensions (Shapes)`}</h3>
    <p>{`For shapes without aspect ratio constraints:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`rect.resize({
  width: 300,
  height: 200,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.contain,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "scale-everything-proportionally"
    }}>{`Scale Everything Proportionally`}</h3>
    <p>{`To scale an element like a zoom operation:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`element.resize({
  width: newWidth,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.proportional,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: false
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "maintain-stroke-appearance"
    }}>{`Maintain Stroke Appearance`}</h3>
    <p>{`To resize while keeping strokes crisp:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`element.resize({
  width: newWidth,
  height: newHeight,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.contain,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "thumbnail-generation"
    }}>{`Thumbnail Generation`}</h3>
    <p>{`To create thumbnails that fit within bounds:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`image.resize({
  width: 150,
  height: 150,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.contain,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "migration-from-deprecated-apis"
    }}>{`Migration from Deprecated APIs`}</h2>
    <p>{`If you're updating code that uses the old (experimental) resize/rescale methods, here are the conversions:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// OLD:
element.rescaleProportionalToWidth(300);

// NEW:
element.resize({
  width: 300,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.proportional,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: false
});

// OLD:
element.rescaleProportionalToHeight(200);

// NEW:
element.resize({
  height: 200,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.proportional,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: false
});

// OLD:
element.resizeToFitWithin(300, 200);

// NEW:
element.resize({
  width: 300,
  height: 200,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.contain,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
});

// OLD:
element.resizeToCover(300, 200);

// NEW:
element.resize({
  width: 300,
  height: 200,
  behavior: constants.ResizeBehavior.cover,
  avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible: true
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "faqs"
    }}>{`FAQs`}</h2>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-how-do-i-resize-an-element"
    }}>{`Q: How do I resize an element?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`node.resize()`}</inlineCode>{` with options specifying width/height, behavior, and visual detail preferences.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-how-do-i-resize-proportionally-by-width"
    }}>{`Q: How do I resize proportionally by width?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`node.resize({ width, behavior: ResizeBehavior.proportional, avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible })`}</inlineCode>{` to maintain aspect ratio.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-whats-the-difference-between-contain-and-cover-behavior"
    }}>{`Q: What's the difference between contain and cover behavior?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Contain fits the element within bounds; cover ensures the element completely covers the specified area.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-what-does-avoidscalingvisualdetailsifpossible-do"
    }}>{`Q: What does avoidScalingVisualDetailsIfPossible do?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` When true, tries to preserve stroke widths, corner radii, and font sizes at their original values.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-can-i-resize-with-only-one-dimension"
    }}>{`Q: Can I resize with only one dimension?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Yes, use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`proportional`}</inlineCode>{` behavior (which requires only one dimension) or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`contain`}</inlineCode>{` behavior with just width or height specified.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-how-do-i-resize-pages"
    }}>{`Q: How do I resize pages?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Set `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`page.width`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`page.height`}</inlineCode>{` properties directly on the PageNode.`}</p>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
;
